note: CURATED public-safety reference — NOT from the E. coli monitoring dataset. These are locally-known drowning / dangerous-swimming locations on the Lower American River. Coordinates are APPROXIMATE; edit this file to refine locations or add hazards. E. coli status and these physical hazards are independent: water can be bacteriologically 'Good' and still be deadly to swim.

hazards.name: Clay Banks
hazards.desc: A popular but hazardous swimming and cliff-area spot. Steep underwater drop-offs, deceptively swift current, and cold snowmelt water have contributed to multiple drownings here.
hazards.tips: Wear a life jacket. Cold water triggers an involuntary gasp reflex and rapid muscle fatigue even for strong swimmers. Never swim alone, and avoid swimming after drinking alcohol.

hazards.name: Tiscornia Beach / Discovery Park Confluence
hazards.type: River confluence · converging currents · sudden depth
hazards.desc: Where the Lower American River meets the larger, colder Sacramento River. Converging currents, abrupt depth changes just offshore, and cold water make this one of the most dangerous swimming areas in the Sacramento region, with a long history of drownings.
hazards.tips: Currents here pull swimmers from ankle-deep water into deep, fast channels within a few steps. Life jackets are strongly advised. Keep children within arm's reach at all times near the confluence.
